heading: A family that built a hearing-care life, not just a hearing-care business.
intro: InnerScope's roots run through a small Central California audiology practice founded by Marvin Posey in the 1950s. That practice grew over decades into more than seventy clinics across California and Florida, built on a single principle: treat every patient like a neighbor, focus on real outcomes, and build long-term relationships.
body: The next generation grew up inside that environment. Mark and Kim Moore raised their family in and around the family clinics, the fitting rooms, the workbenches, the quiet rhythm of a profession measured one patient at a time. Their son Matthew, today InnerScope's CEO, spent his childhood watching adjustments being made and outcomes being explained.

When the over-the-counter hearing-aid category opened up nationally, that operational understanding traveled with the family into the public markets. The clinic-floor instinct, that affordability matters, simplicity matters, and dignity matters, still anchors how InnerScope chooses what to build.

photos.alt: Marvin Posey, founder of the family hearing-aid practice, photographed in the 1950s with an early audiometer.
photos.caption: Marvin Posey with one of the early audiometers used in the family practice, Central Valley California, 1950s.

photos.alt: Mark and Kim Moore with their two young sons inside the family hearing-aid clinic, 1989. A young Matthew Moore is seated at the front of the photo.
photos.caption: Mark and Kim Moore with their children, including a young Matthew Moore, at the family hearing clinic, 1989.
